grant_cycles.funder: European Commission (Horizon Europe / Marie Skłodowska-Curie Actions)
grant_cycles.notes: HIGHEST PRIORITY. Call launched 9 April 2026 with €399.05M budget (~1,600 projects). Open to researchers of any nationality holding a PhD. Mobility rule: must not have resided in the host country for >12 months in the 36 months before deadline. This means the researcher CANNOT host at an Italian institution (has been in Italy since Nov 2023), but CAN host at a UK institution (Cardiff connection via Prof Huggins — was visiting Aug 2025–Jan 2026 which is within the 12-month limit so CHECK carefully), Dutch institution (Groningen connection), or elsewhere. PhD must be obtained by deadline; the researcher's defence is expected Q1 2027 BUT applicants who have 'successfully defended' are also eligible — CHECK whether thesis submission by Oct 2026 suffices. New for 2026: resubmission restrictions apply for scores below 80%. Fellowships typically start mid-2027, which aligns perfectly with the researcher's timeline.

MAJOR CHANGE for 2027: eligibility window expanded to 0–10 years post-PhD (from previous 2–7 years). This means the researcher becomes eligible immediately after PhD defence. However, ERC StG is extremely competitive and typically requires significant track record (independent publications, evidence of research leadership). The researcher's current publication record (3 publications, with one in a strong journal — BIES) may be below the typical competitive threshold. Best used as a long-term target (2028 or 2029 cycle) while building track record. Worth monitoring the call opening in July 2026 to understand new format requirements. Up to €1.5M for 5 years. Any nationality eligible; must spend ≥50% of working time in an EU/Associated Country.

grant_cycles.typical_deadline: Pre-proposal: early September; Full proposal: late January following year
grant_cycles.notes: Pre-proposal for Veni 2026 round expected early September 2026, with full proposals due January 2027. Eligibility: PhD obtained within 3 years of reference date (likely 1 January 2026). The researcher's PhD will not be awarded by that reference date (expected Q1 2027), which is problematic. However, PhDs obtained between the reference date and the pre-proposal deadline may qualify — CHECK timing carefully. Requires an 'embedding guarantee' from a Dutch host institution — the Groningen connection (MSc Economic Geography) could help secure this. Up to €320,000 for 3 years. The researcher's MSc from Groningen is a strong asset for securing institutional support.

The 2026 round closed 19 February 2026. Next round expected February 2027. CRITICAL ELIGIBILITY ISSUE: Applicants must either (a) hold a degree (any degree) from a UK higher education institution, OR (b) hold a UK academic position for ≥4 months prior to the closing date. The researcher does NOT hold a UK degree. The Cardiff research visit (Aug 2025–Jan 2026) may not constitute a 'UK academic position' in the required sense. Unless the researcher secures a UK academic post by ~October 2026, this route is likely BLOCKED. Must also have submitted PhD thesis by the deadline. Internal university deadlines typically Nov–Dec 2026. Awards are 3 years, tenable at UK universities. ~145 fellowships per year.

3-year fellowship in humanities/social sciences at UK institutions. Call opens 1 July 2026, outline deadline 30 September 2026. Eligibility: viva between 1 April 2024 and 1 April 2027. The researcher's expected defence (Q1 2027) falls within window. NATIONALITY REQUIREMENT: Must be UK/EEA national, have UK doctorate, OR demonstrate 'strong prior association' with UK academic community. As an Indonesian national without a UK degree, the researcher would need to argue 'strong prior association' via the Cardiff visit. This is a CHECK — many universities require internal EoIs by May–June 2026. UCL internal deadline is 9 June 2026. Fellowships start Autumn 2027.

grant_cycles.funder: UK Economic and Social Research Council (UKRI)
grant_cycles.notes: NOT ELIGIBLE. The 2026 round deadline is 1 June 2026. Critical requirement: applicants must have 'completed their PhD at a UK research organisation' that is part of an ESRC Doctoral Training Partnership. The researcher's PhD is from Politecnico di Milano (Italy), which disqualifies them. This scheme is specifically for UK PhD graduates.

grant_cycles.funder: UK Economic and Social Research Council (UKRI)
grant_cycles.notes: Open call (rolling deadline), grants of £100,000–£350,000 for up to 5 years. Requires being based at a UK research organisation eligible for ESRC funding. The researcher would need to first secure a UK academic position. Social sciences must represent >50% of research — this fits. Good medium-term target once the researcher has a UK base. Note: some institutions run internal selection (e.g. Cambridge had deadline 13 March 2026). This could be combined with a postdoctoral position or fellowship at a UK institution.

Round 11 opened 2 February 2026 with deadline 16 June 2026. Up to 4+3 years funding (£300K–£2M+). Open to early career researchers of any nationality, but must be supported by a UK host institution. Most universities have already completed internal selection processes for Round 11 (EoIs were due Jan–Feb 2026). MISSED for Round 11. However, Round 12 (expected 2027) is a viable target. No fixed post-PhD time limit, but must justify early career status. The researcher should start building a relationship with a UK host department NOW for Round 12.

grant_cycles.programme: Horizon Europe Cluster 2 — Culture, Creativity and Inclusive Society (2026 calls)
grant_cycles.notes: Cluster 2 calls for 2026 launching 12–15 May 2026 with €298.5M budget. Topics include tackling child poverty and socio-economic transformations — highly relevant to the researcher's work on childcare policy (PNRR Piano Asili Nido). These are collaborative research projects (not individual fellowships), but the researcher could join or help develop a consortium proposal. A strong pathway would be to connect with a PI at Polimi or another institution preparing a bid and contribute spatial microsimulation / policy impact assessment expertise.

New initiative aimed at tackling brain drain and research career precarity. Pilot launched October 2025 (€22.5M); larger call of €51.25M planned for 2027. Co-funds recruitment of postdoctoral researchers for up to 5 years. Call for applications expected to open 8 December 2026. As a non-EU national working in EU research, the researcher may fit the 'attracting global talent' dimension of this initiative. Monitor closely — details still emerging.

2-year fellowships supporting incoming postdoctoral researchers at ETH Zurich. Open to all nationalities. Requires a mentor (ETH professor). The next deadline is 1 September 2026. Highly prestigious with ~40–50% of fellows securing professorships. The researcher would need to identify a suitable mentor in economic geography, regional science, or related field at ETH. Worth exploring as a complementary application alongside MSCA-PF.

upcoming_deadlines.action_needed: START IMMEDIATELY. (1) Identify host institution and supervisor — Cardiff/Prof Huggins is most natural given the existing relationship, but CHECK mobility rule carefully (6-month visit Aug 2025–Jan 2026 should be under the 12-month threshold). Alternatively, target Netherlands (Groningen), other UK universities, or other EU countries where the researcher has NOT resided. (2) Draft research proposal (10-page Part B). (3) Many universities have internal EoI deadlines — e.g., King's College London was 6 May 2026, Oxford History was 14 May 2026. Contact target hosts ASAP. (4) PhD defence timing: applicants who have 'successfully defended' are eligible even without formal degree — verify this applies if the researcher's viva is after the September deadline.

LIKELY MISSED — most institutional internal selections completed Jan–Feb 2026. If by any chance the researcher has a connection to a UK institution that still has a slot, a rapid EoI could be attempted, but this is very unlikely at this stage. Focus on Round 12 preparation instead.

Internal EoI deadlines at UK universities are happening NOW (UCL Political Science: 9 June 2026; UCL Constitution Unit: 25 May 2026). The funder outline deadline is 30 September 2026. The researcher should (1) assess eligibility under the 'strong prior association' criterion — the Cardiff research visit may qualify; (2) contact potential mentors at target UK departments immediately; (3) prepare a concise project proposal. The fellowship is 3 years starting Autumn 2027.

Contact University of Groningen (Faculty of Spatial Sciences or similar) to discuss hosting and the embedding guarantee. Begin drafting narrative CV (evidence-based CV format required by NWO) and research idea (100–500 words). Critical question: will the PhD timeline allow eligibility? If the reference date is 1 January 2026, PhDs awarded between that date and the pre-proposal deadline should qualify — but the researcher's PhD may not be awarded until Q1 2027. Verify with NWO directly.

Call expected to open July 2026. Under the new rules, the researcher is eligible immediately after PhD defence (0–10 year window from 2027). However, the researcher will likely not have defended by the October deadline. If defence happens before the deadline, an application is technically possible but the track record may not yet be competitive enough for ERC. RECOMMENDED: use the 2027 call documents as preparation for a future (2028/2029) application. Begin developing the proposal concept now.

upcoming_deadlines.programme: Horizon Europe Cluster 2 — Social and Economic Transformations 2026
upcoming_deadlines.deadline: 2026-05-15 (call opening) — submission deadlines vary by topic, likely Sep–Oct 2026
upcoming_deadlines.action_needed: Review the 9 topics under Cluster 2 Destination 3 when they are published on 15 May 2026. Topics on tackling child poverty and social/economic transformations are highly relevant. The researcher should alert their PI / supervisor at Polimi and explore consortium participation. These are collaborative grants, not individual, so the researcher would join as a team member or work package leader.

Identify a potential ETH Zurich mentor in economic geography, urban economics, or spatial analysis. Draft a research proposal that builds on PhD work but proposes a new direction. Contact potential mentor well in advance. Open to any nationality.

The 2027 round funder deadline is expected February 2027. University-level internal EoIs typically due November–December 2026. CRITICAL: the researcher must either (a) hold a UK degree (does NOT), or (b) hold a UK academic position for ≥4 months before the deadline. Unless the researcher secures a UK-based academic post by approximately October 2026, they are NOT eligible. If a short-term post or visiting position at Cardiff or another UK university can be arranged, this becomes viable. Discuss with Prof Huggins urgently.

strategy_recommendations: IMMEDIATE PRIORITY (May–June 2026): Focus all energy on the MSCA Postdoctoral Fellowship 2026 application (deadline 9 September 2026). This is the single best match for the researcher's profile — open to any nationality, any field, well-funded (€399M budget for ~1,600 grants), and the researcher's international mobility profile (Indonesia → Netherlands → Italy → UK visit) is exactly what MSCA rewards. The start date (mid-2027) aligns perfectly with the funding gap after PNRR expiry in October 2026.

HOST INSTITUTION STRATEGY FOR MSCA-PF: The Cardiff connection with Prof Robert Huggins is the most developed relationship and should be the primary target. Cardiff University (School of Geography and Planning) is a strong host for economic geography / regional economics research. Verify that the 6-month visit (Aug 2025–Jan 2026) does not breach the 12-month mobility rule — it should be fine since it is well under 12 months within the 36-month window. As a backup, approach University of Groningen (economic geography department where the MSc was obtained — BUT note the mobility rule means the Netherlands may be problematic if the researcher resided there for the MSc). Also consider approaching departments at other UK or EU institutions that have strong regional economics groups.

PARALLEL TRACK — BRITISH ACADEMY PDF: Apply in parallel to the British Academy Postdoctoral Fellowship (outline deadline 30 September 2026). The eligibility is tighter — the researcher must argue 'strong prior association with the UK academic community' via the Cardiff visit. This is not guaranteed but is worth attempting. The BA PDF is a 3-year fellowship, highly prestigious in social sciences, and would start Autumn 2027. Reach out to potential UK mentors NOW as internal deadlines at many universities are May–June 2026.

PhD TIMING IS CRITICAL: Many schemes require PhD to be completed by the application deadline. MSCA-PF requires a PhD 'at the time of the deadline' (9 September 2026), but applicants who have 'successfully defended' without formal award are also eligible. The researcher must either (a) accelerate submission/defence to before September 2026, or (b) verify that the 'successfully defended' clause covers their situation. This single factor could determine eligibility for MSCA-PF, Veni, and ERC StG. Discuss with the PhD supervisor immediately about the possibility of submitting the thesis by July/August 2026 and scheduling an early defence.

GRONINGEN CONNECTION FOR NWO VENI: If the PhD timeline permits, the NWO Veni (pre-proposal September 2026) is a strong secondary option. The researcher's MSc from Groningen establishes a natural link to the Netherlands. Contact the Faculty of Spatial Sciences or relevant department at Groningen to explore hosting. Veni provides €320,000 over 3 years and is well-suited for researchers transitioning to independence. The SSH panel structure at NWO favours applied social science with policy relevance.

BUILD THE PUBLICATION PIPELINE URGENTLY: With 3 publications (2 journal articles + 1 book chapter), the researcher's record is adequate for MSCA-PF but below the competitive threshold for ERC StG or Leverhulme ECF. Before October 2026: (a) submit at least 1 new paper from PhD research (preferably a methods-focused paper on spatial microsimulation or a policy-focused paper on Italian childcare), (b) aim to have a working paper or preprint available by the MSCA-PF deadline, (c) target journals like Regional Studies, Journal of Regional Science, or Environment and Planning to signal disciplinary identity in economic geography.

LONG-TERM POSITIONING (2027–2028): If MSCA-PF or BA PDF succeeds, use the fellowship period to build toward an ERC Starting Grant application (2028 or 2029 cycle). The new 0–10 year eligibility window from 2027 means the researcher has a long runway. Focus on publishing 3–4 additional papers, securing a pilot dataset for the ERC proposal, and building a distinctive research identity around 'spatial microsimulation for ex-ante policy assessment' which sits at a productive intersection of methods innovation and policy impact.

UKRI FUTURE LEADERS FELLOWSHIP: Although Round 11 is effectively missed, Round 12 (expected 2027) is a strong medium-term target. Start building relationships with potential UK host departments now. The FLF provides up to 7 years of funding and has no post-PhD time limit — only requiring that the applicant justify early career status. The researcher's profile (regional economics + spatial methods + policy evaluation) fits well within the ESRC remit portion of UKRI.

SAFETY NET — CONSIDER ITALIAN/EU RTDa or ASSEGNO DI RICERCA POSITIONS: If competitive fellowships do not materialise by October 2026, apply for short-term research positions (assegno di ricerca) at Italian universities or RTDa (fixed-term researcher) positions to bridge the funding gap while continuing to apply for fellowships. Polimi and other Italian departments working on PNRR evaluation may have continuing positions.

LEVERAGE THE CARDIFF VISIT FOR MAXIMUM EFFECT: The research visit to Cardiff (Aug 2025–Jan 2026) should be framed not merely as a visit but as the foundation of a longer-term research collaboration. In all applications, present this as evidence of international research independence, a cross-cultural comparative dimension (Italian childcare policy in comparative European context), and a strong supervisory relationship that will continue into the postdoctoral phase.

emerging_opportunities.opportunity: Horizon Europe Cluster 2 — 2026 calls on child poverty and social/economic transformations (€298.5M budget, opening 12–15 May 2026)
emerging_opportunities.source: European Research Executive Agency announcement (Dec 2025) and Horizon Europe 2026-27 Work Programme
emerging_opportunities.relevance: Directly aligned with the researcher's PhD on Italian childcare expansion (PNRR Piano Asili Nido). The 2026 Cluster 2 topics explicitly mention 'tackling child poverty' and 'socio-economic transformations'. While these are collaborative grants (not individual fellowships), the researcher could join a consortium, contributing spatial microsimulation and ex-ante policy evaluation expertise. This is both a funding opportunity and a networking/CV-building opportunity.

emerging_opportunities.opportunity: Choose Europe for Science initiative — €51.25M call planned for 2027 (applications expected to open 8 December 2026)
emerging_opportunities.source: Horizon Europe 2026-27 Work Programme and Science|Business reporting
emerging_opportunities.relevance: New EU instrument aimed at 'tackling brain drain and research career precarity' by co-funding postdoctoral recruitment for up to 5 years. As a non-EU researcher working in European research, the researcher is precisely the target audience for the 'attracting global talent' strand. Details are still emerging — monitor closely as the call specification develops.

emerging_opportunities.opportunity: ERC eligibility expansion from 2027 — Starting Grants now open 0–10 years post-PhD
emerging_opportunities.source: ERC announcement on changes to 2026 and 2027 Work Programmes (erc.europa.eu)
emerging_opportunities.relevance: Previously, ERC StG required 2–7 years post-PhD. The expansion to 0–10 years means the researcher can apply immediately after PhD defence, dramatically expanding the strategic window. This makes the ERC StG a realistic medium-term target rather than something to consider only 2+ years after PhD.

emerging_opportunities.opportunity: New European Partnership on Social Transformations (Horizon Europe Cluster 2)
emerging_opportunities.relevance: A new European partnership dedicated to 'social transformations, the future of work, essential services and the just transition.' Childcare as an essential service and labour market participation of parents are directly relevant. This partnership will likely launch calls that value exactly the researcher's expertise in ex-ante policy impact assessment.

emerging_opportunities.opportunity: Horizon Europe simplified application processes and two-stage evaluations
emerging_opportunities.source: European Commission Work Programme 2026-27; Science|Business analysis
emerging_opportunities.relevance: The 2026-27 Work Programme features less prescriptive topics (reduced topic length by 33%), wider use of two-stage applications (41 calls), and lump-sum funding for half of calls. This reduces the initial application burden and makes it more feasible for early-career researchers to participate in collaborative bids.

emerging_opportunities.opportunity: NWO Talent Programme undergoing evaluation — potential reforms for 2027
emerging_opportunities.relevance: NWO is conducting a comprehensive evaluation of the Veni/Vidi/Vici programmes, expected to complete September 2026. Potential reforms include increased success rates (target 25%) and broader recognition of non-traditional outputs in evidence-based CVs. If reforms materialise, the Veni 2027 round could be more accessible.

cv_gaps_to_address: PUBLICATION VOLUME: With 3 publications (2 journal articles, 1 book chapter), the researcher's output is below the competitive threshold for most prestigious fellowships. Target: submit at least 1–2 new papers by September 2026. Priority paper 1: a methodological paper on the spatial microsimulation framework (SimJogja or the Italian childcare model). Priority paper 2: a policy-focused paper on PNRR childcare expansion with preliminary findings. Even preprints/working papers strengthen the MSCA-PF application.

INDEPENDENT PUBLICATION RECORD: Only one publication (BIES 2022) is sole-authored. For ERC StG competitiveness (medium-term target), the researcher needs multiple publications without the PhD supervisor. Aim for at least 2 sole-authored or first-authored papers from the PhD before the end of 2026.

LACK OF UK INSTITUTIONAL AFFILIATION: Many UK schemes (Leverhulme ECF, ESRC PDF, BA PDF) require either a UK degree or UK academic position. The researcher has neither. MITIGATION: (a) frame the Cardiff research visit as 'prior association with the UK academic community' for BA PDF; (b) explore the possibility of a short-term visiting research position or honorary affiliation at Cardiff to strengthen the UK connection; (c) long-term, if aiming for UK-based career, securing any UK postdoctoral position (even short-term) opens multiple fellowship doors.

RESEARCH INDEPENDENCE NARRATIVE: The researcher needs to articulate a clear, independent research agenda that goes beyond the PhD topic. For MSCA-PF and BA PDF: develop a 'next step' project that builds on (but is distinct from) the PhD. E.g., extending spatial microsimulation from Italian childcare to broader European welfare policy comparison, or developing a digital twin framework for ex-ante policy assessment.

INTERNATIONAL NETWORK DOCUMENTATION: The researcher has connections in Indonesia (UGM, Bank Indonesia, IRSA), Netherlands (Groningen), Italy (Polimi), UK (Cardiff), and Japan (Nagoya). These need to be explicitly documented and leveraged in applications. For MSCA-PF, the international network is a scoring criterion. Create a clear map of collaborators and their potential roles in the proposed research.

TEACHING EXPERIENCE: Most UK fellowships (Leverhulme ECF, BA PDF) expect evidence of teaching capability. If the researcher has not yet accumulated teaching experience at Polimi, seek opportunities to give guest lectures, supervise student projects, or co-teach a module before October 2026.

GRANT WRITING EXPERIENCE: The researcher has no track record of winning competitive funding as PI. For UKRI FLF or ERC StG, this matters less for very early career, but for NWO Veni or ESRC NIG, demonstrable ability to manage a research budget strengthens the application. Consider applying for small grants (e.g., conference organisation funding, seed grants from Polimi) to build this track record.

DIGITAL TWIN / COMPUTATIONAL SKILLS DOCUMENTATION: The researcher lists 'digital twin integration' as a skill but this needs to be evidenced in publications or demonstrable projects. If the PhD involves a digital twin component, ensure this is clearly documented (even as a working paper or technical report) as it is a highly fundable skill across multiple EU programmes.
